item.title: Misa cosplay (Death Note)
item.description: Inspired by Sozomaika's iconic art of her! I tried to recreate it as well as possible, the hardest part was setting up the lighting in a way that shows up on the clothes but not too much that the background turns purple (: *other socials: https://instagram.com/anna_himejima https://reddit.com/u/aniku_

item.title: Jeez, sorry to disappoint ....
item.description: In 1960, Frankie Lymon performed “Little Bitty Pretty One” on national television, and many Southern viewers were shocked to discover he was Black—having previously only heard his voice on the radio and assumed he was white. The live audience’s visibly stunned reaction exposed the racial biases of the time. Despite his popularity, the moment highlighted the challenges Black artists faced in gaining equal recognition and acceptance in mainstream media. It became a powerful reflection of how race shaped perceptions in early American pop culture!
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: The Dump Before Time 14: My 14 Phone-Clearing Dump of Random
item.description:
I mean, let's at least hear them out.
Let's kick it off with a bang.
Vodka?
That's some sweet ink.
Take a hint.
Alone or in pairs.
Frosty!
Take it easy.
Sly Cooper.
Take a whiff.
I can smell it from here.
Ingenuity.
Smooth.
A little off target.
Ingenuity 2.
Looks good.
A mystery.
Fun for the whole family.
And you get to clean it all up!
PC Load Letter?
Don't mind if I do.
Dealing with Karens must be hard.
Have a spare.
Stealth.
Somehow I think I'd do the same.
Probably just a squirrel.
There is a difference.
He was just in the neighborhood.
Let's get an early start on this.
And Ozzy Osbourne got arrested for doing something similar. Where's his plaque?
Dress appropriately.
The void is sneaky.
Spread them cheeks.
They don't grow on trees.
It's been a long winter.
Goals.
That's no ceiling.
Going down.
Something about trade or whatever.
No, no, he's got a point.
Catch them all.
What are you doing, step teacher?
Wants to battle.
Flap gums too much you do.
Way to go, Cody.
I see no issue.
Relate.
Did you do it right?
Seriously.
Take a hint.
What would the customers think.
Maybe speed up?
We're on to you.
Again, a long winter.
I'm no botanist.
They're stuffed in there good.
Sometimes once is all it takes.
Back At Fork.
I demand this for my wake.
Party on.
It knows what it's doing.
New saint just dropped.
It explains a lot.
Not much progress has been made, honestly.
Ready for action.
Manscape.
Losers.
He don't need no education.
He's even got his coffee.
Started making trouble in my neighborhood.
It's art.
I did this years ago.
For defense.
Modern solutions.
It's cute.
Yeah, Pooh.
The right side looks good.
Taco cat is just taco cat backwards.
From Mexico, With Love.
Paradise.
That's so beautifully evil.
I've done this.
Take aim.
Yup.
Yeah, I'm kind of like this.
No comment.
Hard at work.
*golf clap*
Tastes like air.
Turn it off and on again.
They weren't even as long back then.
Amazing.
Everyone gets one.
I'm always grateful.
That's nice.
Weeks, even.
Maybe it's for soup or something.
A little late on this one.
Lzzy tax!
Void tax!

Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos

item.title: A Day in the Life...(Frank edition)
item.description:
Woke up this morning and felt like crud, so I worked from home. Frank was very understanding. The white fuzzy rope is now Frank's new nemesis.
He found the regular food and decided to try some grown up cat food.
I dropped a spoon and Frank was not amused. It took him about a minute to calm down. And you know, a minute in human time is like five minutes in cat time.
The giant cat tree was climbed. And then promptly abandoned.
This is where he spent most of the day, helping me code one handed. Hope you guys are having a good day.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: I got COVID, so I made my best Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe!
item.description: https://www.triedandtruerecipe.com/my-best-chicken-noodle-soup/ I knew I wasn't feeling well, so I figured I'd make a pot of chicken soup since I had a little tickle in my throat. Today, I wake up feeling like death...do a flu test, and lo and behold...positive after avoiding it for 5 years!! At least I'm set for the week cuz I made such a big pot! I used broad egg noodles and ALL chicken thighs (instead of a whole chicken) this time since it's all I had, but it was still delicious and perfect for days when you're feeling under the weater. Chicken broth: 5-6 pound whole chicken (giblets reserved for another use) 1 teaspoon salt per pound of chicken 1 yellow onion (peeled and quartered) 2 ribs celery (cut into thirds) 6-8 sprigs thyme 1 head garlic (top cut off; cloves left in the paper) 12-14 cups water Chicken noodle soup: 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil 1 yellow onion (about 4 ounces, peeled and diced; see Note 1) 4 ribs celery (trimmed and diced) 4 carrots (trimmed, peeled, and cut into half-moons) 8 cloves garlic (peeled and minced) 2 teaspoons dry thyme 10 cups homemade broth from above; see Note 2 4-5 cups shredded or diced chicken from the homemade broth above 1 10.5- ounce can condensed cream of chicken soup 1 cup fresh dill (loosely packed finely chopped; or use parsley or a mix) 12 ounces dry egg noodles (broad or thin, depending on preference) Salt and pepper Make the broth: Place the chicken in a large pot. Season with 1 teaspoon salt per pound of chicken. Add the onion, celery, thyme, and garlic. Cover with water, ensuring the chicken is submerged if your pot allows it. You will need 12–14 cups of water to cover the chicken. It’s ok if the very top of the bird is exposed. Turn the heat on the pot to high and bring to a boil. Cover and reduce the heat to low. Simmer for at least 1 hour 30 minutes or until the chicken is completely cooked. Using tongs, carefully transfer the chicken to a large bowl and cool for 10-15 minutes. Turn the heat on the pot to medium-high. Allow the broth to simmer rapidly, uncovered, for 30 minutes, reducing it to 10 cups total. It will deepen in color and flavor. Adjust the heat as needed to keep the broth from reducing too much. Strain the broth: Carefully strain the broth through a sieve into a large bowl. Discard the spent herbs and vegetables. Wipe out the pot and return it to the stove. Pick the chicken: Pick the meat from the chicken, saving the carcass and skin for stock if you like. Shred the meat with your fingers or dice it into cubes if preferred. Set aside. Make the soup: Heat 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil in the pot over medium heat. Add the onion, celery, and carrots. Sauté for 5–7 minutes. Add the garlic and 2 teaspoons dry thyme. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant. Pour in all but 1 cup of the homemade broth and add the cooked chicken. Bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low. Cover and simmer for 30–40 minutes or until the vegetables are fork-tender. Taste and adjust the seasonings to your preference. Be mindful not to add too much salt during this step, as you’ll finish the soup with the condensed cream of chicken soup. Temper the condensed cream of chicken soup: Whisk the cream of condensed chicken soup with the 1 cup reserved broth until smooth. Set aside. Finish the soup: Meanwhile, bring a large pot of water to a boil. Cook the noodles according to package directions. Drain and rinse. Add the cooked noodles and condensed soup mixture to the soup. Add the finely chopped herbs. Turn off the heat and let stand for 10 minutes. Taste and adjust the seasonings to your preference. To serve: Ladle the soup into bowls. Garnish with black pepper and enjoy! Notes Note 1: You will need 4 cups total of onion, celery, and carrots. You can adjust the proportions to your liking. Depending on the size of your carrots or ribs of celery, you may need one or two more than the recipe recommends. Note 2: The liquid should be reduced to about 10 cups of broth. You may have a little more or a little less, depending on the intensity of your simmer. The soup is very forgiving! If it reduces significantly, add a bit of water to reach 10 cups of broth. If you have a little extra broth, adding it to the soup is okay. Try not to add too much more than 10 cups of broth.
item.dc:creator: triedandtruerecipes
